title: Level 8.2
wikitext:
{{Foreground|File=Test.gif|opacity=0.1}} {{CSS|User:IceCraft2022/level 8.2.css}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 8.2: '''"Xanthous Caverns"'''}}{{Blurbackground|file=8.2.png}}
{{Blurquote|image=8.2.png|quote-text=
{{Class 1|color-h=30|hexagon=❄|class=5|safety=Unsafe & Unstable|security=Environmental Hazards|entity=High Entity Count|text-color=FFFFFF}}
{{Blurquote|quote-text= '''Description''' |image=8.2.png}}
{{Blurimage|file=8.2.png|caption=A captured photo of Level 8.2, in one of its more unstable sections. Note the appearance of snow ontop the pathway.}} {{Blurimage|file=caves.PNG|caption=An upclose of Level 8.2's icicles.}} {{Blurimage|file=the fear of yellow.PNG|caption=A general image of Level 8.2.}} '''Level 8.2''', otherwise known as '''"Xanthous Caverns"''', is one of the first documented sub-levels situated between '''[[Level 8]]''' and '''[[Level 9]]'''. This level is confirmed to be directly connected with '''[[Level 8]]''' as both levels depict a large, subterranean space, as well as the presence of multiple entities which frequent to inhabit areas of the level, with the only differences which separate '''Level 8.2''' from '''[[Level 8]]''', being the true hazards of the level more focused on the level's environmental properties instead of the entities themselves. '''Level 8.2''', reportedly discovered on the date of 28 January 2007, is an expansive underground cave system with a large array of tunnels, crawlspaces, etcetera, similarly to its parent level, '''[[Level 8]]'''; hence its memorable designation. The main differences which set it greatly apart from '''[[Level 8]]''' are the presence of an alleged "light" in the supposed exterior of the level at all times, which allow it to have a greater amount of illumination within through multiple microscopic crevices, yet does not heat up the level itself. Furthermore, the cluttered presence of non-anomalous snow can be found across all parts of '''Level 8.2''', maintaining the level at a fluctuating frigid -15°C to -21°C, meaning one can easily suffer from hypothermia. Another feature which set it apart from its parent level is the presence of a characteristic, currently unknown kind of yellow stone replacing standard rock within the caves, giving all surroundings (including snow) a yellow color and hue. The level is also known to be quite unstable and erratic, leading to the frequency of multiple environmental hazards that can happen unexpectedly, such as abrupt shaking, the collapsing of certain sections found within the cave, among many others. In addition, as one proceeds further and further into the level away from one's initial entry point, audible whispering can be constantly heard in darker sections of the level emanating from an unknown source, speaking in an undetermined and currently unintelligible language. However, under no circumstances should one follow the source of these voices. The level's layout is very large, complex, hollow, and exceedingly labyrinth, to the point of being physically unmappable, as it contains many abnormally narrow paths, tight dead ends, huge hollowed areas with no discernible terrain, and massive pitch black voids which are not recommended to jump into, as they have been preferably unexplored and undocumented. As to make matters worse, the kind of stone '''Level 8.2''''s caves are exclusively built in naturally contain several crevices, slits, holes and bents, seemingly weakening them, proving it to be tough to get around the level without dropping into freefall down into the void below, so it is advised to not cross pathways floating amidst a void. Frequently, the appearance of multiple common terrain formations can be sporadically found across '''Level 8.2''', and generally range from small water bodies of the size in which it can be considered a pond, typically filled with '''[[Object 1|Almond Water]]''', yet are unfortunately contaminated greatly by the '''[[Hydrolitis Plague]]''', large arrays of complicated and rough rocky formations, as well as multiple networks of stalagmites, stalactites, helictites, soda straws, and many more from the same kind, which can typically be observed "purposefully blocking off" certain areas from the wanderer to access. Another differentiable feature found in '''Level 8.2''' is perceived if one were to wander downwards into the darker areas of the level, as these areas are regarded to be sporadically infested with '''[[Entity 4|Deathmoths]]'''. '''[[Entity 4|Deathmoths]]''' are thought to preferably inhabit and set their nests in more empty, void-like areas of the level, as considering the height one would fall down if to trip accidentally, they'd most likely instantly be considered deceased due to injuries sustained, only to then later feast on their corpse. As advice to avoid '''[[Entity 4|Deathmoth]]'''-infested areas, one must look out if the approaching area begins to emit a stench comparable to that of decayed meat, as well as the visible increase of [[Object 6|Moth Jelly]] being splattered across the cave walls and floors.
{{Blurquote|quote-text= '''Entities''' |image=8.2.png}}
It is thought multiple other entities actively lurk around other sections of '''Level 8.2''', yet these are most abundant. ==
'''[[Entity 4|Deathmoths]]'''
== '''[[Entity 4|Deathmoths]]''', as aforementioned, are more strategically aggressive and much more invasive in this level. It is best to avoid them at all costs to prevent further injury. ==
'''Hypno-Spiders'''
== Spider-like entities have been continually reported to lurk among the level closely to '''[[Entity 4|Deathmoths]]''', and appear to not belong to any discernible spider species from '''[[The Frontrooms]]'''. These spiders, however, according to reports, possess a highly psychologically-damaging venom which can be expulsed from their body at any moment if they feel threatened, which essentially hypnotizes wanderers in a way. These entities are proven to be extremely tough and can only be killed by extremely powerful weapons. It is best to avoid them as much as possible.
{{Blurquote|quote-text= '''Hazards''' |image=8.2.png}}
The environmental hazards which can be found in '''Level 8.2''' are too many to be fully documented, so the most persistent ones have been listed. ==
'''A Cold Wash-Over'''
== The "Cold Wash-Over" is an environmentally hazardous event with a fluctuating chance of happening, that if it were handled properly and with caution, may merely cause some disturbance to one's journey, yet can turn into the wanderer's death if handled unproperly. It involves the sudden flood of the current subterranean space the wanderer is in, with water seemingly chasing down the wanderer down the cave system. This drowns the cave system in cold, freezing cold ice water, and if were not obstructed in time, could potentially leave vast parts of the cave to be entirely blocked off with mere ice and water. ==
'''Icicles of Fragility'''
== Another environmental hazard which is more prominent to happen across the level may somewhat relate to the spiky terrain specifically found in the ceiling. Upon initial glance, the stalagmites which are clinging across the ceiling may appear quite durable and toughened against the level's environmental hazards, yet one must not trust their appearance, as according to multiple incidents, they are actually quite fragile and break down effortlessly to the ground, either falling into the holes or cutting off passageways or paths. Although, it seems it specifically occurs on top of unsuspecting wanderers standing right below an array of stalagmites instead, immediately rendering them deceased and temporarily blocks said area of the cave, sometimes leaving other wanderers trapped within and die to starvation. Protective head gear is advised to have equipped before you make entry into '''Level 8.2''', and under any circumstance should one NOT stand below stalagmites.
{{Blurquote|quote-text= '''Colonies & Outposts''' |image=8.2.png}}
Due to the level’s constantly changing hazards and its hostility towards humans becoming more and more apparent, it was preferred to not establish any kind of colony or outpost within '''Level 8.2'''.
{{Blurquote|quote-text='''Discovery Log''' |image=8.2.png}}
John Creickey, who first discovered '''Level 8.2''', decided to contact the base and tried to inform them about what he could discover. The log is attached below.
==
COMMENCE LOG
== John: Damn, base. I didn't think that '''[[Level 8]]''' is so cold. It's literally freezing my legs...
Base: Well, try to find a way out of there as quick as possible. We can't lose another one.
John: R-right... Wait a minute, since when was '''[[Level 8]]''' this cold?
Base: We don't know, John. Just get back to base as soon as possible.
John: Will do- Hold up... What is that in the distance? I see like, another cave, but this time it is more yellowish and has this weird yellow goo on it that looks like cheese. Should I attempt to explore it and get information?
Base: Well, be careful, I guess. From what you said, it's really, really cold over there. Wear suitable clothes or else you'll become true ice this time.
John: I don't have time for jokes, [UNINTELLIGIBLE]. I'll just get straight to work then.
Base: Alright, we'll see the info we can get from this, erm, phenomenon, I guess? Is it a whole level of its own or is it just one area?
John: I... I think its a level of its own.
Base: Kay. Tell us what you see then.
John: Well, I think im starting to hear whispers from the other side of the cave, and '''[[Entity 4|Deathmoths]]''' are flying around like they're trying to get me... I even see some abnormal entities, but I mostly see these weird-looking spiders, they're even expulsing some kind of venom on the walls too, I think.
'''[Static can suddenly begin to be heard]'''
Base: Damnit. Our connection seems to be currently experiencing some errors... We will have to stop the talk for now, John. Take caution when you attempt to explore further, kay?
John: Listened, commander.
'''[A few minutes later]'''
Base: Alright, John, we managed to fix the connection. Are you done yet?
John: Yeah, I think. I've mostly collected lots of valuable data on this level.
Base: Good. Now, uh, if you can, please come back to the base.
John: Please be patient on me though. It's a new level, so I don't even know where the exits are...
Base: Alright then. I've dispatched some other '''[[M.E.G.]]''' members to go look for you.
John: Thanks, I guess.
----
{{Blurquote|quote-text= '''Entrances and Exits''' |image=8.2.png}}
==
'''Entrances'''
== It is thought multiple entrances and exits in and out of '''Level 8.2''' currently exist, yet these are the most well-known. *Colder but brighter places in '''[[Level 8]]''' could lead to '''Level 8.2'''. Hints of it leading to '''Level 8.2''' can be noted by the narrow pathways and giant holes and potholes covered in snow. *Swimming in '''[[Level 7]]''' and going deeper into the sea might lead one to '''Level 8.2''' instead. This exit is reachable without a suit, only requiring to hold your breath for around 3 minutes. But to travel to the direction of the sun may require a raft. If it starts getting colder and it gets more yellowish, these are signs of '''Level 8.2'''. *If a certain, abnormal brightness starts emerging from somewhere in specific in '''[[Level 202.1]]''', reaching the source of this brightness would take you here. ==
'''Exits'''
== *Going through an area or tunnel that emits a dark, thick, pitch black smog will eventually guide one into '''[[Level 9]]'''. *Following the whispering heard in certain dark areas will teleport you to '''[[Level 6]]'''.
